FILE src/NxtUI.Core/Services/JsonPathQueryService.cs
LENGTH 2855
using System.Text.Json;
using System.Text.Json.Nodes;
using Json.Path;

namespace NxtUI.Core.Services;

/// <summary>
/// Result of evaluating a JSONPath (RFC 9535) expression against a JSON document via
/// JsonPath.Net. On success, ResultJson is a JSON array of every matched value (even for
/// a single match) so callers always get one shape to render — matches an empty array,
/// not an error, when the path is syntactically valid but selects nothing.
/// </summary>
public sealed record JsonPathQueryResult(bool Success, string? ResultJson, string? Error, int MatchCount)
{
    public static JsonPathQueryResult Ok(string resultJson, int matchCount) => new(true, resultJson, null, matchCount);
    public static JsonPathQueryResult Fail(string error) => new(false, null, error, 0);
}

/// <summary>
/// Runs a JSONPath query against a raw JSON string — shared by the Kafka message
/// inspector and the Mongo document inspector's detail-panel query bar.
/// </summary>
public static class JsonPathQueryService
{
    private static readonly JsonSerializerOptions SerializeOptions = new() { WriteIndented = false };

    public static JsonPathQueryResult Evaluate(string? json, string? path)
    {
        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(path))
            return JsonPathQueryResult.Fail("Enter a JSONPath expression, e.g. $.store.book[*].title");

        JsonNode? root;
        try
        {
            root = JsonNode.Parse(json ?? "null");
        }
        catch (JsonException ex)
        {
            return JsonPathQueryResult.Fail($"Invalid JSON: {ex.Message}");
        }

        if (root is null)
            return JsonPathQueryResult.Fail("Document is empty.");

        JsonPath parsed;
        try
        {
            parsed = JsonPath.Parse(path);
        }
        catch (PathParseException ex)
        {
            return JsonPathQueryResult.Fail($"Invalid JSONPath: {ex.Message}");
        }

        PathResult result;
        try
        {
            result = parsed.Evaluate(root);
        }
        catch (Exception ex)
        {
            // JsonPath.Net can throw at evaluation time too (e.g. a function called
            // with the wrong argument types) — not just at parse time.
            return JsonPathQueryResult.Fail($"Query failed: {ex.Message}");
        }

        var matches = result.Matches;
        if (matches is null || matches.Count == 0)
            return JsonPathQueryResult.Ok("[]", 0);

        // Matched nodes are still attached to `root` — JsonNode instances can only have
        // one parent, so they must be cloned before going into a new JsonArray.
        var array = new JsonArray();
        foreach (var match in matches)
            array.Add(match.Value?.DeepClone());

        return JsonPathQueryResult.Ok(array.ToJsonString(SerializeOptions), matches.Count);
    }
}
FILE src/NxtUI.Core/Services/LogPathDiscoveryService.cs
LENGTH 7867
using Microsoft.Extensions.Logging;
using Microsoft.Extensions.Options;
using NxtUI.Core.Configuration;
using NxtUI.Core.Logging;
using NxtUI.Core.Models;
using System.Collections.Concurrent;

namespace NxtUI.Core.Services;

public sealed class LogPathDiscoveryService(IOptions<FileBrowserSettings> options, ILogger<LogPathDiscoveryService> log) : ILogPathDiscoveryService
{
    // key → running or completed search task
    private readonly ConcurrentDictionary<string, Task<string?>> _cache = new();

    // key → resolved result, populated once RunSearchAsync completes. GetCachedPath reads
    // from here instead of the task above — a cache-only lookup should never touch a Task's
    // .Result even when it's known-completed (that pattern is easy to accidentally copy
    // somewhere it DOES block; a plain dictionary read can't).
    private readonly ConcurrentDictionary<string, string?> _resolved = new();

    public event Action<string>? OnPathResolved;

    public static string CacheKey(ServiceStatus svc, string env) =>
        $"{env}|{svc.HostName}|{svc.ServiceName}|{svc.ProcessId}";

    public string? GetCachedPath(ServiceStatus svc, string env) =>
        _resolved.TryGetValue(CacheKey(svc, env), out var path) ? path : null;

    public bool IsSearching(ServiceStatus svc, string env) =>
        _cache.TryGetValue(CacheKey(svc, env), out var task) && !task.IsCompleted;

    public void EnsureDiscovering(ServiceStatus svc, string env)
    {
        var key = CacheKey(svc, env);
        var added = false;
        _cache.GetOrAdd(key, _ => { added = true; return RunSearchAsync(svc, env, key); });
        if (added)
            log.LogTrace("discovery [{Env}]: starting search for {Svc}@{Host} pid={Pid}", env, svc.ServiceName, svc.HostName, svc.ProcessId);
    }

    public async Task<string?> FindNowAsync(ServiceStatus svc, string env)
    {
        var key = CacheKey(svc, env);

        // Reuse any in-progress search
        if (_cache.TryGetValue(key, out var existing) && !existing.IsCompleted)
            return await existing;

        // Start a fresh search (replaces a completed null result from a previous attempt)
        var task = RunSearchAsync(svc, env, key);
        _cache[key] = task;
        return await task;
    }

    public Task<string?> FindServiceParentFolderAsync(ServiceStatus svc, string env) =>
        Task.Run(() =>
        {
            foreach (var template in options.Value.ServiceTemplates)
            {
                // Drop the path segment containing {pid} entirely — not just the PID
                // itself — so this doesn't depend on any specific instance having ever
                // logged (the parent, e.g. "{service}_{env}" for the day, is expected to
                // always exist once the service has run at all that day).
                var pidIdx = template.IndexOf("{pid}", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
                if (pidIdx < 0) continue;
                var slashBeforePid = template.LastIndexOf('\\', pidIdx);
                if (slashBeforePid < 0) continue;

                var parentTemplate = template[..slashBeforePid];
                var expanded = ExpandTemplate(parentTemplate, svc, env);
                var resolved = ResolveWildcard(expanded);
                if (resolved is not null) return resolved;
            }
            return null;
        });

    public void PruneStaleEntries(string env, IReadOnlySet<string> activeKeys)
    {
        var prefix = env + "|";
        var removed = 0;
        foreach (var key in _cache.Keys.Where(k => k.StartsWith(prefix, StringComparison.Ordinal) && !activeKeys.Contains(k)).ToList())
        {
            if (_cache.TryRemove(key, out _)) removed++;
            _resolved.TryRemove(key, out _);
        }
        if (removed > 0)
            log.LogDebug("discovery [{Env}]: pruned {Count} stale cache entr{Suffix}", env, removed, removed == 1 ? "y" : "ies");
    }

    public void ClearEnv(string env)
    {
        var prefix = env + "|";
        foreach (var key in _cache.Keys.Where(k => k.StartsWith(prefix, StringComparison.Ordinal)).ToList())
            _cache.TryRemove(key, out _);
        foreach (var key in _resolved.Keys.Where(k => k.StartsWith(prefix, StringComparison.Ordinal)).ToList())
            _resolved.TryRemove(key, out _);
    }

    // ── Internal ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    private async Task<string?> RunSearchAsync(ServiceStatus svc, string env, string key)
    {
        var sw = System.Diagnostics.Stopwatch.StartNew();
        string? result;
        try
        {
            result = await Task.Run(() => SearchSync(svc, env));
        }
        catch (Exception ex)
        {
            log.LogError(ex, "discovery [{Env}]: search failed for {Svc}@{Host} pid={Pid} after {Ms}ms",
                env, svc.ServiceName, svc.HostName, svc.ProcessId, sw.ElapsedMilliseconds);
            throw;
        }

        _resolved[key] = result;

        if (result is not null)
        {
            log.LogTrace("discovery [{Env}]: found folder for {Svc}@{Host} -> {Path} in {Ms}ms",
                env, svc.ServiceName, svc.HostName, result, sw.ElapsedMilliseconds);
            OnPathResolved?.Invoke(key);
        }
        else
        {
            log.LogTrace("discovery [{Env}]: no folder found for {Svc}@{Host} pid={Pid} after {Ms}ms",
                env, svc.ServiceName, svc.HostName, svc.ProcessId, sw.ElapsedMilliseconds);
        }
        return result;
    }

    private string? SearchSync(ServiceStatus svc, string env)
    {
        foreach (var template in options.Value.ServiceTemplates)
        {
            var expanded = ExpandTemplate(template, svc, env);
            log.LogTrace("discovery [{Env}]: trying {Expanded}", env, expanded);
            var resolved = ResolveWildcard(expanded);
            if (resolved is not null) return resolved;
        }
        return null;
    }

    private static string ExpandTemplate(string template, ServiceStatus svc, string env) =>
        LogPathTemplate.Expand(template, svc, env);

    /// <summary>
    /// Resolves a path that may contain * wildcards in one or more segments.
    /// Recursively expands each wildcard segment via Directory.GetDirectories.
    /// </summary>
    private static string? ResolveWildcard(string path)
    {
        if (!path.Contains('*'))
            return Directory.Exists(path) ? path : null;

        var starIdx = path.IndexOf('*');
        var slashPrev = path.LastIndexOf('\\', starIdx);
        if (slashPrev < 0) return null;

        var basePath = path[..slashPrev];
        var slashNext = path.IndexOf('\\', starIdx);

        string segment, remainder;
        if (slashNext < 0)
        {
            segment = path[(slashPrev + 1)..];
            remainder = string.Empty;
        }
        else
        {
            segment = path[(slashPrev + 1)..slashNext];
            remainder = path[slashNext..]; // includes leading backslash
        }

        if (!Directory.Exists(basePath)) return null;

        string[] matches;
        try { matches = Directory.GetDirectories(basePath, segment); }
        catch { return null; }

        // Pick the most-recently-modified directory first so active log sessions
        // win over stale folders from previous runs that sort earlier alphabetically.
        matches = [.. matches.OrderByDescending(Directory.GetLastWriteTimeUtc)];

        foreach (var match in matches)
        {
            var candidate = string.IsNullOrEmpty(remainder) ? match : match + remainder;
            var result = ResolveWildcard(candidate);
            if (result is not null) return result;
        }
        return null;
    }
}
FILE src/NxtUI.Core/Services/OperationLog.cs
LENGTH 1962
using System.Diagnostics;
using Microsoft.Extensions.Logging;

namespace NxtUI.Core.Services;

/// <summary>
/// Uniform start/duration/error logging for I/O-bound operations (Kafka, SQL, disk
/// search, parsing, ...) so long-running or failing operations are easy to find by
/// grepping logs for "failed after" or "completed in" across every domain, instead of
/// each service inventing its own ad-hoc timing.
/// </summary>
public static class OperationLog
{
    /// <summary>Completions at or above this take LogWarning instead of LogDebug, so
    /// slow operations stand out from routine ones without raising the whole category's
    /// log level.</summary>
    public static readonly TimeSpan SlowThreshold = TimeSpan.FromSeconds(3);

    public static async Task<T> RunAsync<T>(ILogger log, string action, Func<Task<T>> operation)
    {
        log.LogDebug("{Action}: started", action);
        var sw = Stopwatch.StartNew();
        try
        {
            var result = await operation();
            LogCompleted(log, action, sw.Elapsed);
            return result;
        }
        catch (OperationCanceledException)
        {
            log.LogDebug("{Action}: cancelled after {Ms}ms", action, sw.ElapsedMilliseconds);
            throw;
        }
        catch (Exception ex)
        {
            log.LogError(ex, "{Action}: failed after {Ms}ms", action, sw.ElapsedMilliseconds);
            throw;
        }
    }

    public static Task RunAsync(ILogger log, string action, Func<Task> operation) =>
        RunAsync(log, action, async () => { await operation(); return true; });

    private static void LogCompleted(ILogger log, string action, TimeSpan elapsed)
    {
        if (elapsed >= SlowThreshold)
            log.LogWarning("{Action}: completed in {Ms}ms (slow)", action, (long)elapsed.TotalMilliseconds);
        else
            log.LogDebug("{Action}: completed in {Ms}ms", action, (long)elapsed.TotalMilliseconds);
    }
}
FILE src/NxtUI.Core/Services/PerformanceEventStore.cs
LENGTH 2642
using NxtUI.Core.Models;

namespace NxtUI.Core.Services;

/// <summary>
/// In-memory event store that accumulates PerformanceEvents
/// keyed by (name, service, processId).
/// Thread-safe for concurrent polling/updates.
/// </summary>
public class PerformanceEventStore
{
    private readonly object _lock = new();
    private readonly Dictionary<string, PerformanceEvent> _events = new();
    private DateTime? _lastEventTimestamp;

    /// <summary>
    /// Returns a read-only snapshot of all events.
    /// </summary>
    public IReadOnlyDictionary<string, PerformanceEvent> Snapshot
    {
        get
        {
            lock (_lock)
            {
                return new Dictionary<string, PerformanceEvent>(_events);
            }
        }
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// Total number of unique events in the store.
    /// </summary>
    public int Count
    {
        get
        {
            lock (_lock)
            {
                return _events.Count;
            }
        }
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// Timestamp of the most recent event, or null if empty. Maintained incrementally
    /// on upsert rather than scanning every event on each access — safe because a key's
    /// timestamp only ever moves forward (see UpsertEvent) and keys are never removed
    /// except by Clear(), so the running max stays correct without a rescan.
    /// </summary>
    public DateTime? LastEventTimestamp
    {
        get { lock (_lock) return _lastEventTimestamp; }
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// Upsert or merge an event by its composite key.
    /// If the key exists, the newer event (by timestamp) replaces it.
    /// </summary>
    public void UpsertEvent(PerformanceEvent evt)
    {
        if (evt is null) return;

        lock (_lock)
        {
            if (!_events.TryGetValue(evt.Id, out var existing) || evt.Timestamp > existing.Timestamp)
            {
                _events[evt.Id] = evt;
                if (_lastEventTimestamp is null || evt.Timestamp > _lastEventTimestamp)
                    _lastEventTimestamp = evt.Timestamp;
            }
        }
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// Merge multiple events at once (batch upsert).
    /// </summary>
    public void UpsertEvents(IEnumerable<PerformanceEvent> events)
    {
        if (events is null) return;

        foreach (var evt in events)
        {
            UpsertEvent(evt);
        }
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// Clear all events (for testing or reset).
    /// </summary>
    public void Clear()
    {
        lock (_lock)
        {
            _events.Clear();
            _lastEventTimestamp = null;
        }
    }
}
FILE src/NxtUI.Core/Services/TopologyComputationService.cs
LENGTH 16962
using NxtUI.Core.Models;

namespace NxtUI.Core.Services;

/// <summary>
/// Computes a flow graph topology from the in-memory event store.
/// Infers service nodes with pipeline rows, and pipeline-to-pipeline edges,
/// per design doc §8.2.
/// </summary>
public class TopologyComputationService(TimeSpan? recentActivityWindow = null)
{
    /// <summary>
    /// Recent-activity window used to decide whether a pipeline is "Active"
    /// (priority 2) vs merely "InProgress" (priority 3). A pipeline counts
    /// as Active if it has had a finish event within this window of the
    /// latest known event time. Configurable via RunsSettings.TopologyRecentActivityWindowSeconds
    /// — callers should pass that value in; defaults to 15s if omitted (e.g. in tests).
    /// </summary>
    private readonly TimeSpan _recentActivityWindow = recentActivityWindow ?? TimeSpan.FromSeconds(15);

    /// <summary>
    /// Computes topology from the event store snapshot.
    /// </summary>
    /// <param name="labelFormatter">
    /// Optional display-label shortener applied to node labels and pipeline display names
    /// (see <see cref="TopologyLabelFormatter"/>). Never affects the raw <c>Id</c>/<c>Name</c>
    /// keys used for edge matching. Null = identity (no shortening).
    /// </param>
    public Topology ComputeTopology(
        IReadOnlyDictionary<string, PerformanceEvent> eventStore,
        Func<string, string>? labelFormatter = null,
        TopologyBlueprint? blueprint = null)
    {
        var format = labelFormatter ?? (s => s);

        if (eventStore == null || eventStore.Count == 0)
        {
            // Even with no events, a blueprint still renders its skeleton (declared services
            // greyed as NotStarted) so the expected flow shows from t=0.
            var empty = new Topology { TotalChunks = 0, TotalEvents = 0 };
            if (blueprint is not null) ApplyBlueprint(empty, blueprint, format);
            return empty;
        }

        var topology = new Topology { TotalEvents = eventStore.Count };

        var events = eventStore.Values.ToList();
        var latestEventTime = events.Max(e => e.Finish ?? e.Start);

        // ── Phase 1: group events by (service, pipeline) ────────────────
        var byServicePipeline = events
            .GroupBy(e => (e.Service, e.Pipeline))
            .ToDictionary(g => g.Key, g => g.ToList());

        var nodesByService = new Dictionary<string, TopologyNode>();
        var pipelineRowsByKey = new Dictionary<(string Service, string Pipeline), PipelineRow>();

        foreach (var ((service, pipeline), pipelineEvents) in byServicePipeline)
        {
            if (!nodesByService.TryGetValue(service, out var node))
            {
                node = new TopologyNode { Id = service, Label = format(service), IsObserved = true };
                nodesByService[service] = node;
            }

            var row = BuildPipelineRow(pipeline, pipelineEvents, latestEventTime, format, _recentActivityWindow);
            node.Pipelines.Add(row);
            pipelineRowsByKey[(service, pipeline)] = row;
        }

        // ── Phase 2: per-node rollups (instance count, header state) ────
        foreach (var node in nodesByService.Values)
        {
            node.Pipelines = node.Pipelines.OrderBy(p => p.Name, StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase).ToList();

            node.InstanceCount = node.Pipelines
                .SelectMany(p => p.Instances)
                .Select(i => $"{i.Server}:{i.ProcessId}")
                .Distinct()
                .Count();

            node.HeaderState = node.Pipelines.Count == 0
                ? PipelineState.NotStarted
                : node.Pipelines.Max(p => p.State); // enum ordered so highest = highest priority
        }

        topology.Nodes = nodesByService.Values.OrderBy(n => n.Label, StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase).ToList();

        var allNames = events.Select(e => e.Name).Distinct().ToHashSet();
        topology.TotalChunks = allNames.Count;
        topology.TotalDone = events.Count(e => e.IsDone);
        // Every event is either done or not — no need for a second full scan.
        topology.TotalInProgress = events.Count - topology.TotalDone;

        // ── Phase 3: infer pipeline-to-pipeline edges from chunk flow ────
        // For each chunk, order the (service, pipeline) hops it visited by
        // start time. Consecutive distinct hops become directed edges.
        var eventsByChunk = events
            .GroupBy(e => e.Name)
            .ToDictionary(g => g.Key, g => g.OrderBy(e => e.Start).ToList());

        var edgesByKey = new Dictionary<(string, string, string, string), TopologyEdge>();

        // Track, per (service,pipeline), the set of names that have a
        // *finished* event there — used for the waiting estimate below.
        var doneChunksByPipeline = byServicePipeline.ToDictionary(
            kv => kv.Key,
            kv => kv.Value.Where(e => e.IsDone).Select(e => e.Name).ToHashSet());

        var anyEventChunksByPipeline = byServicePipeline.ToDictionary(
            kv => kv.Key,
            kv => kv.Value.Select(e => e.Name).ToHashSet());

        foreach (var chunkEvents in eventsByChunk.Values)
        {
            (string Service, string Pipeline)? prevHop = null;

            foreach (var evt in chunkEvents)
            {
                var hop = (evt.Service, evt.Pipeline);

                if (prevHop.HasValue && prevHop.Value != hop)
                {
                    var key = (prevHop.Value.Service, prevHop.Value.Pipeline, hop.Service, hop.Pipeline);
                    if (!edgesByKey.TryGetValue(key, out var edge))
                    {
                        edge = new TopologyEdge
                        {
                            Source = prevHop.Value.Service,
                            SourcePipeline = prevHop.Value.Pipeline,
                            Target = hop.Service,
                            TargetPipeline = hop.Pipeline,
                            IsObserved = true,
                        };
                        edgesByKey[key] = edge;
                    }
                    edge.DoneCount++;
                }

                prevHop = hop;
            }
        }

        // ── Phase 4: waiting estimate per edge ───────────────────────────
        // waiting ≈ count of names where source pipeline has finish set
        // but target pipeline has no event yet (§8.2).
        foreach (var edge in edgesByKey.Values)
        {
            var sourceKey = (edge.Source, edge.SourcePipeline);
            var targetKey = (edge.Target, edge.TargetPipeline);

            var sourceDone = doneChunksByPipeline.GetValueOrDefault(sourceKey, new HashSet<string>());
            var targetSeen = anyEventChunksByPipeline.GetValueOrDefault(targetKey, new HashSet<string>());

            edge.WaitingEstimate = sourceDone.Count(c => !targetSeen.Contains(c));

            // Edge colour inherits the source pipeline row's state.
            edge.State = pipelineRowsByKey.TryGetValue(sourceKey, out var sourceRow)
                ? sourceRow.State
                : PipelineState.NotStarted;
        }

        topology.Edges = edgesByKey.Values
            .OrderBy(e => e.Source, StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
            .ThenBy(e => e.SourcePipeline, StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
            .ThenBy(e => e.Target, StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
            .ThenBy(e => e.TargetPipeline, StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
            .ToList();

        // ── Phase 5: overall estimated progress (§8.3) ──────────────────
        // Denominator: max hop count observed across chunks (i.e. number of
        // distinct (service,pipeline) nodes in the longest observed chain)
        // × total unique names seen.
        var maxHops = eventsByChunk.Values.Count == 0
            ? 0
            : eventsByChunk.Values.Max(chunkEvents =>
                chunkEvents.Select(e => (e.Service, e.Pipeline)).Distinct().Count());

        var denominator = (double)maxHops * allNames.Count;
        var numerator = (double)events.Count(e => e.IsDone);

        topology.EstimatedProgress = denominator > 0
            ? Math.Clamp(numerator / denominator, 0.0, 1.0)
            : 0.0;

        if (blueprint is not null) ApplyBlueprint(topology, blueprint, format);

        return topology;
    }

    // ── Blueprint merge (run-type topology hints) ──────────────────────────────
    // Advisory overlay: decorate observed nodes with hint metadata, add greyed skeleton
    // nodes/edges for declared-but-unseen services, flag observed-but-undeclared nodes, and
    // attach the layout. Runtime data always wins on state/counts — this only adds structure.
    private static void ApplyBlueprint(Topology topology, TopologyBlueprint blueprint, Func<string, string> format)
    {
        topology.Layout = blueprint.Layout;
        topology.HasBlueprint = true;
        topology.GroupColors = new Dictionary<string, string>(blueprint.GroupColors, StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);

        // Declared names/globs are matched against each node's *stripped* form (the same
        // env-agnostic display label produced by `format`), never the raw event.Service id.
        // This lets one literal declared name match the same logical service across every
        // environment even though the raw id embeds an env-specific token.
        var nodesById = topology.Nodes.ToDictionary(n => n.Id, StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
        var strippedSeen = new HashSet<string>(topology.Nodes.Select(n => format(n.Id)), StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);

        // 1. Decorate observed nodes with the first matching ServiceHint (declaration order).
        foreach (var node in topology.Nodes)
        {
            var hint = blueprint.Services.FirstOrDefault(s => Glob.IsMatch(format(node.Id), s.Name));
            if (hint is not null) Decorate(node, hint);
            // else: observed but undeclared → IsDeclared stays false (dashed border in the UI).
        }

        // 2. Skeleton nodes for LITERAL declared services never seen (globs have no concrete
        //    identity to draw before the service appears, so they only decorate — step 1).
        foreach (var hint in blueprint.Services)
        {
            if (hint.IsGlob || strippedSeen.Contains(hint.Name)) continue;
            var node = new TopologyNode
            {
                Id = hint.Name,
                Label = format(hint.Name),
                HeaderState = PipelineState.NotStarted,
                IsObserved = false,
            };
            Decorate(node, hint);
            topology.Nodes.Add(node);
            nodesById[node.Id] = node;
            strippedSeen.Add(hint.Name);
        }

        // 3. Declared edges: mark matching observed edges, or add greyed skeleton edges between
        //    declared endpoints that resolve to concrete nodes.
        var existing = new HashSet<string>(
            topology.Edges.Select(e => $"{e.Source}{e.Target}"), StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);

        // Endpoints are resolved against each node's stripped form too, so edges declared
        // with env-agnostic names line up with the (possibly env-suffixed) real node ids.
        var idsByStrippedForm = topology.Nodes
            .Select(n => n.Id)
            .ToLookup(id => format(id), StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);

        foreach (var (from, to) in blueprint.DeclaredEdges)
        foreach (var src in ResolveNodes(from, idsByStrippedForm))
        foreach (var dst in ResolveNodes(to, idsByStrippedForm))
        {
            if (string.Equals(src, dst, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)) continue;
            var key = $"{src}{dst}";
            if (existing.Contains(key))
            {
                foreach (var e in topology.Edges)
                    if (string.Equals(e.Source, src, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) &&
                        string.Equals(e.Target, dst, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
                        e.IsDeclared = true;
            }
            else
            {
                topology.Edges.Add(new TopologyEdge
                {
                    Source = src, Target = dst,
                    SourcePipeline = string.Empty, TargetPipeline = string.Empty,
                    State = PipelineState.NotStarted, IsDeclared = true, IsObserved = false,
                });
                existing.Add(key);
            }
        }
    }

    private static void Decorate(TopologyNode node, ServiceHint hint)
    {
        node.IsDeclared = true;
        node.Role = hint.Role;
        node.Group = hint.Group;
        node.Color = hint.Color;
        node.Order = hint.Order;
        node.Pin = hint.Pin;
        node.PinX = hint.PinX;
        node.PinY = hint.PinY;
    }

    // A declared endpoint (literal or glob) → the concrete node ids it resolves to, matched
    // against each node's stripped form (ids grouped by that form via idsByStrippedForm).
    private static IEnumerable<string> ResolveNodes(string nameOrGlob, ILookup<string, string> idsByStrippedForm)
    {
        var isGlob = nameOrGlob.Contains('*') || nameOrGlob.Contains('?');
        return isGlob
            ? idsByStrippedForm.Where(g => Glob.IsMatch(g.Key, nameOrGlob)).SelectMany(g => g)
            : idsByStrippedForm[nameOrGlob];
    }

    // ── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    private static PipelineRow BuildPipelineRow(
        string pipeline, List<PerformanceEvent> pipelineEvents, DateTime latestEventTime,
        Func<string, string> format, TimeSpan recentActivityWindow)
    {
        // Single pass instead of four separate Count() scans over the same list.
        int done = 0, inProgress = 0, errors = 0, recentDone = 0;
        foreach (var e in pipelineEvents)
        {
            if (e.IsDone) done++; else inProgress++;
            if (e.IsError) errors++;
            if (e.Finish.HasValue && (latestEventTime - e.Finish.Value) <= recentActivityWindow) recentDone++;
        }

        var row = new PipelineRow
        {
            Name = pipeline,
            DisplayName = format(pipeline),
            Topic = InferTopicName(pipeline),
            DoneCount = done,
            InProgressCount = inProgress,
            ErrorCount = errors,
            Progress = (done + inProgress) > 0 ? (double)done / (done + inProgress) : 0.0,
        };

        // Recent throughput: fraction of "done" events that finished within
        // the recent-activity window of the latest known event time.
        row.RecentThroughputScore = done > 0
            ? Math.Clamp((double)recentDone / Math.Max(done, 1), 0.0, 1.0)
            : 0.0;

        row.State = DetermineState(row, recentDone);

        // Per-instance breakdown, sorted by server then PID per §8.2.
        row.Instances = pipelineEvents
            .GroupBy(e => (e.Server, e.ProcessId))
            .Select(g => new InstanceStats(
                server: g.Key.Server,
                processId: g.Key.ProcessId,
                doneCount: g.Count(e => e.IsDone),
                inProgressCount: g.Count(e => !e.IsDone),
                errorCount: g.Count(e => e.IsError),
                lastActivity: g.Max(e => e.Finish ?? e.Start)))
            .OrderBy(i => i.Server, StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
            .ThenBy(i => i.ProcessId)
            .ToList();

        return row;
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// Priority order per §8.2 (1 = highest):
    /// 1. Errored, 2. Active, 3. InProgress, 4. Idle, 5. Completed, 6. NotStarted.
    /// </summary>
    private static PipelineState DetermineState(PipelineRow row, int recentDone)
    {
        if (row.ErrorCount > 0) return PipelineState.Errored;
        if (row.InProgressCount == 0 && row.DoneCount == 0) return PipelineState.NotStarted;
        if (recentDone > 0) return PipelineState.Active;
        if (row.InProgressCount > 0) return PipelineState.InProgress;
        if (row.DoneCount > 0 && row.InProgressCount == 0) return PipelineState.Completed;
        return PipelineState.Idle;
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// Derives a plausible Kafka topic name from a pipeline name for the
    /// hover-tooltip link (§8.2). The real topic name will come from the
    /// backend once available — this is a presentation-layer placeholder.
    /// </summary>
    private static string InferTopicName(string pipeline) =>
        string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(pipeline) ? string.Empty : pipeline.ToLowerInvariant().Replace(' ', '-') + "-events";
}
FILE src/NxtUI.Core/Services/TopologyHintLoader.cs
LENGTH 1997
using System.Collections.Concurrent;
using System.Text.Json;
using Microsoft.Extensions.Logging;
using NxtUI.Core.Models;

namespace NxtUI.Core.Services;

/// <summary>
/// Loads optional per-run-type topology hints from <c>{BasePath}/topology/{runType}.json</c>
/// (see <see cref="EnvironmentConfigOptions.BasePath"/>). Files are parsed once and cached
/// for the process lifetime; a missing/invalid file resolves to null (the graph then falls
/// back to pure runtime inference — hints are advisory). Lookup key is the run's Type,
/// lowercased; the flow shape doesn't vary by environment, so this is not env-scoped.
/// </summary>
public sealed class TopologyHintLoader(EnvironmentConfigOptions options, ILogger<TopologyHintLoader> log)
{
    private static readonly JsonSerializerOptions _jsonOptions = new()
    {
        PropertyNameCaseInsensitive = true,
        ReadCommentHandling = JsonCommentHandling.Skip,
        AllowTrailingCommas = true,
    };

    // Cached including the "not found" result (null) so we don't stat the disk on every poll.
    private readonly ConcurrentDictionary<string, TopologyHintFile?> _cache =
        new(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);

    /// <summary>Returns the hint file for a run type, or null if none exists / failed to parse.</summary>
    public TopologyHintFile? Get(string? runType)
    {
        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(runType)) return null;
        return _cache.GetOrAdd(runType, Load);
    }

    private TopologyHintFile? Load(string runType)
    {
        var path = Path.Combine(options.BasePath, "topology", $"{runType.ToLowerInvariant()}.json");
        if (!File.Exists(path)) return null;

        try
        {
            var json = File.ReadAllText(path);
            return JsonSerializer.Deserialize<TopologyHintFile>(json, _jsonOptions);
        }
        catch (Exception ex)
        {
            log.LogError(ex, "Failed to load topology hint from {Path}", path);
            return null;
        }
    }
}
FILE src/NxtUI.Core/Services/TopologyLabelFormatter.cs
LENGTH 2252
using System.Text.RegularExpressions;

namespace NxtUI.Core.Services;

/// <summary>
/// Shortens service/pipeline labels for display in the run-detail flow graph by removing
/// configured filler words (see <c>UiSettings.GraphLabelStripWords</c>). This affects the
/// rendered <c>Label</c>/<c>DisplayName</c> only — never the raw names used as topology and
/// edge-matching keys.
/// </summary>
public static class TopologyLabelFormatter
{
    // Collapse a run of 2+ separators (left behind after a word is cut out of the middle
    // of a name, e.g. "Ccr_AXIS-DEV1_Incr" -> "Ccr__Incr") down to a single separator.
    private static readonly Regex DoubledSeparators = new(@"[_-]{2,}", RegexOptions.Compiled);

    /// <summary>
    /// Removes each of <paramref name="words"/> (case-insensitive) from <paramref name="raw"/>,
    /// substituting the current environment id for the <c>{EnvID}</c> token, then collapses
    /// doubled separators and trims leading/trailing <c>-</c>/<c>_</c>/spaces. Never returns an
    /// empty string — if stripping would remove everything, the original is kept.
    /// </summary>
    public static string Strip(string? raw, IReadOnlyList<string>? words, string? envId)
    {
        if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(raw)) return raw ?? string.Empty;
        if (words is null || words.Count == 0) return raw;

        var result = raw;
        foreach (var word in words)
        {
            var actual = word == "{EnvID}" ? envId : word;
            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(actual)) continue;
            result = Regex.Replace(result, Regex.Escape(actual), string.Empty, RegexOptions.IgnoreCase);
        }

        // Collapse "__" / "--" / "-_" runs to the run's first character, then trim edges.
        result = DoubledSeparators.Replace(result, m => m.Value[0].ToString());
        result = result.Trim(' ', '-', '_');

        // Guard against a name made entirely of strip-words collapsing to nothing.
        return result.Length == 0 ? raw : result;
    }

    /// <summary>Convenience factory: a reusable formatter closed over a word list and env id.</summary>
    public static Func<string, string> Build(IReadOnlyList<string>? words, string? envId) =>
        raw => Strip(raw, words, envId);
}

// Periodic memory-metrics sample published by each service instance to the
// per-environment metrics topic (single partition, low retention). Tailed
// live by ServiceMetricsMonitor; disk-based log parsing only backfills
// history older than the topic's retention window.
message MetricsTracker {

  string ServiceInstanceId = 2;
  google.protobuf.Timestamp ProcessStartTime = 3;
  string HostName = 4;
  int64 CurrentMemoryUsageByte = 5;
  int64 PeakMemoryUsageByte = 6;
  int64 ChildProcessesMemoryUsageByte = 7;
  int64 ChildProcessesPeakMemoryUsageByte = 8;
}

FILE src/NxtUI.Protos/Protos/ProtoMsg.proto
LENGTH 818
syntax = "proto3";
option csharp_namespace = "NxtUI.Protos";
package nxtui;

// Bitmask flags. Wire format is identical to a plain int32 (varint), so
// declaring this as an enum instead doesn't change what's on the wire —
// it just lets the JSON viewer decompose combinations like 3 into
// "ARCHIVED | URGENT" instead of showing a bare number.
enum ProtoMsgFlags {
  PROTO_MSG_FLAGS_NONE      = 0;
  PROTO_MSG_FLAGS_ARCHIVED  = 1;
  PROTO_MSG_FLAGS_URGENT    = 2;
  PROTO_MSG_FLAGS_REPROCESS = 4;
}

// Generic envelope used when no specific type matches a topic.
// Services typically set type_url to a fully-qualified type name and
// place the actual serialised proto in payload.
message ProtoMsg {
  string type_url = 1;
  bytes  payload  = 2;
  map<string, string> metadata = 3;
  ProtoMsgFlags flags = 4;
}

FILE src/NxtUI.Web/Components/BmPagination.razor

@namespace NxtUI.Web.Components

<div class="bm-pagination-wrap">
    <MudPagination Count="@Count"
                   Selected="@Selected"
                   SelectedChanged="@(async (int p) => await SelectedChanged.InvokeAsync(p))"
                   ShowFirstButton="true"
                   ShowLastButton="true"
                   Size="Size.Small"
                   Class="bm-pagination-ctrl" />

    @if (Count > JumpThreshold)
    {
        @if (_editing)
        {
            <MudNumericField T="int" @bind-Value="_editValue"
                              Min="1" Max="@Count"
                              Immediate="true"
                              AutoFocus="true"
                              Variant="Variant.Outlined" Margin="Margin.Dense"
                              Class="bm-pagination-jump-input"
                              @onkeydown="OnKeyDown"
                              @onblur="@(() => CommitJump())" />
        }
        else
        {
            <MudTooltip Text="Click to jump to a page">
                <span class="bm-pagination-jump-label" @onclick="StartEditing">
                    page @Selected / @Count
                </span>
            </MudTooltip>
        }
    }
</div>

@code {
    [Parameter, EditorRequired] public int Count { get; set; }
    [Parameter, EditorRequired] public int Selected { get; set; }
    [Parameter, EditorRequired] public EventCallback<int> SelectedChanged { get; set; }

    // Only show the "jump to page" control once there are enough pages that
    // clicking through them one at a time would be tedious.
    private const int JumpThreshold = 7;

    private bool _editing;
    private int  _editValue;

    private void StartEditing()
    {
        _editValue = Selected;
        _editing   = true;
    }

    private async Task OnKeyDown(KeyboardEventArgs e)
    {
        if (e.Key == "Enter") await CommitJump();
        else if (e.Key == "Escape") _editing = false;
    }

    private async Task CommitJump()
    {
        if (!_editing) return;
        _editing = false;
        var target = Math.Clamp(_editValue, 1, Math.Max(1, Count));
        if (target != Selected)
            await SelectedChanged.InvokeAsync(target);
    }
}

@code {
    [Parameter, EditorRequired] public List<RunSummary> Rows { get; set; } = new();
    [Parameter] public bool Loading { get; set; }
    [Parameter] public bool FixedHeader { get; set; } = true;

    // When false, the Actions column (open detail / timeline / stop) is omitted —
    // used by pickers like the Timeline "Add Run" dialog where rows are selected,
    // not acted on directly.
    [Parameter] public bool ShowActions { get; set; } = true;

    // Row-level single-click selection (distinct from the per-cell OnOpenDetail
    // double-click), and a per-row CSS class hook for highlighting the selection.
    [Parameter] public EventCallback<RunSummary> OnRowClick { get; set; }
    [Parameter] public Func<RunSummary, int, string>? RowClassFunc { get; set; }

    // When false (Home's "recent runs" use case), no footer/pagination is shown
    // and no header sort links are wired — just the same columns/actions as
    // the full Runs page, with whatever rows the caller already fetched.
    [Parameter] public bool ShowPaginationAndFilter { get; set; } = true;

    // When false, the internal pagination/rows-per-page footer is suppressed while
    // sorting (ShowPaginationAndFilter) still works — used when the caller wants to
    // render pagination itself elsewhere (e.g. the Timeline "Add Run" dialog puts it
    // in the dialog's action bar instead of under the table).
    [Parameter] public bool ShowFooter { get; set; } = true;

    [Parameter] public int TotalCount { get; set; }
    [Parameter] public int Page { get; set; } = 1;
    [Parameter] public int PageSize { get; set; } = 25;
    [Parameter] public int TotalPages { get; set; } = 1;
    [Parameter] public EventCallback<int> PageChanged { get; set; }
    [Parameter] public EventCallback<int> PageSizeChanged { get; set; }

    // Server-side sort state — only meaningful when ShowPaginationAndFilter is true.
    [Parameter] public string? SortField { get; set; }
    [Parameter] public bool SortDescending { get; set; }
    [Parameter] public EventCallback<string> OnSort { get; set; }

    [Parameter, EditorRequired] public EventCallback<RunSummary> OnOpenDetail { get; set; }
    [Parameter] public EventCallback<RunSummary> OnOpenTimeline { get; set; }
    [Parameter] public EventCallback<RunSummary> OnStopRun { get; set; }

    private RenderFragment HeaderContent(string label, string field)
    {
        if (!ShowPaginationAndFilter || !OnSort.HasDelegate)
        {
            return @<text>@label</text>;
        }

        return @<MudLink Underline="Underline.Hover"
                 Color="Color.Inherit"
                 OnClick="@(() => OnSort.InvokeAsync(field))"
                 Style="cursor:pointer; display:inline-flex; align-items:center; gap:2px;">
            @label
            @if (SortField == field)
            {
                <MudIcon Icon="@(SortDescending ? Icons.Material.Filled.ArrowDownward : Icons.Material.Filled.ArrowUpward)"
                          Size="Size.Small" />
            }
        </MudLink>;
    }

    private static string FormatDuration(TimeSpan ts) =>
        ts.TotalHours >= 1
            ? $"{(int)ts.TotalHours:D2}:{ts.Minutes:D2}:{ts.Seconds:D2}"
            : $"{ts.Minutes:D2}:{ts.Seconds:D2}";
}
FILE src/NxtUI.Web/Components/BulkActionDialog.razor
LENGTH 10232
@namespace NxtUI.Web.Components
@using System.Text.Json
@inject IJSRuntime JS
@inject NavigationManager Nav
@inject ISnackbar Snackbar
@inject ErrorNotificationService Notifications
@implements IAsyncDisposable

<MudDialog>
    <TitleContent>@ActionVerb @Kind(true)</TitleContent>
    <DialogContent>
        @if (_loadingItems)
        {
            <MudProgressLinear Indeterminate="true" Color="Color.Primary" Class="mb-2" />
        }
        else
        {
            <MudTextField T="string" Value="@_filterText" ValueChanged="OnFilterChanged"
                          Label="Filter (wildcards: * and ?)" Placeholder="e.g. *test*"
                          Immediate="true" AutoFocus="true" Disabled="@_running" Class="mb-2" />

            <div class="bm-restart-svc-list">
                @if (_matches.Count == 0)
                {
                    <div class="bm-muted bm-restart-svc-empty">No matching @Kind(false).</div>
                }
                else
                {
                    @foreach (var name in _matches)
                    {
                        var result = _results.GetValueOrDefault(name);
                        var failed = result is { Success: false };
                        <div class="bm-restart-svc-row @(result is not null ? (result.Success ? "bm-purge-row-ok" : "bm-purge-row-fail bm-purge-row-clickable") : "")"
                             title="@(failed ? result!.Message : null)"
                             @onclick="@(() => CopyErrorAsync(failed ? result!.Message : null))">
                            <span class="bm-restart-svc-name">@name</span>
                            @if (result is not null)
                            {
                                <MudIcon Icon="@(result.Success ? Icons.Material.Filled.CheckCircle : Icons.Material.Filled.Error)"
                                         Size="Size.Small" Color="@(result.Success ? Color.Success : Color.Error)"
                                         Title="@(failed ? "Click to copy the error" : null)" />
                            }
                        </div>
                    }
                }
            </div>

            @if (_running || _completed is not null)
            {
                <MudProgressLinear Value="@_progressPercent" Color="Color.Warning" Class="mt-2" />
                <div class="bm-muted bm-purge-progress-label">
                    @if (_completed is { } c)
                    {
                        <span>Done — @c.SuccessCount succeeded, @c.FailCount failed (of @c.Total).</span>
                        @if (c.FailCount > 0)
                        {
                            <span class="bm-purge-hint"> Hover a failed row to see why; click it to copy the error.</span>
                        }
                    }
                    else
                    {
                        <span>@(ActionVerb)ing @_doneCount / @_matches.Count…</span>
                    }
                </div>
            }

            @if (_error is not null)
            {
                <MudAlert Severity="Severity.Error" Dense="true" Class="mt-2">@_error</MudAlert>
            }
        }
    </DialogContent>
    <DialogActions>
        <MudButton OnClick="@(() => MudDialog.Close(DialogResult.Ok(true)))" Disabled="@_running">
            Close
        </MudButton>
        <MudButton Color="@ButtonColor" Variant="Variant.Filled"
                   Disabled="@(_matches.Count == 0 || _running || _loadingItems)"
                   OnClick="RunAsync">
            @if (_running)
            {
                @($"{ActionVerb}ing…")
            }
            else if (_completed is not null)
            {
                @($"{ActionVerb} again ({_matches.Count})")
            }
            else
            {
                @($"{ActionVerb} ({_matches.Count})")
            }
        </MudButton>
    </DialogActions>
</MudDialog>

@code {
    [CascadingParameter] IMudDialogInstance MudDialog { get; set; } = default!;

    /// <summary>Verb shown in the title/button/progress text, e.g. "Restart" / "Purge".</summary>
    [Parameter] public string ActionVerb { get; set; } = "Run";

    /// <summary>Label used in messages, e.g. "service" / "Kafka topic" / "Mongo collection".</summary>
    [Parameter, EditorRequired] public string ItemLabel { get; set; } = "item";

    /// <summary>Supplies the full candidate list to filter — may itself be async (e.g. a fresh Mongo query).</summary>
    [Parameter, EditorRequired] public Func<Task<List<string>>> LoadItemsAsync { get; set; } = () => Task.FromResult(new List<string>());

    [Parameter] public Color ButtonColor { get; set; } = Color.Warning;

    /// <summary>NDJSON endpoint (relative to the app's base URI) to POST the request to.</summary>
    [Parameter] public string Endpoint { get; set; } = "api/bulk-action-simulate";

    /// <summary>Builds the request body from the currently filtered item list. Defaults to
    /// the simulate endpoints' {Action, Kind, Items} shape; real endpoints that need extra
    /// context (e.g. Mongo's Env/Database) supply their own.</summary>
    [Parameter] public Func<List<string>, object>? BuildRequestBody { get; set; }

    private string Kind(bool titleCase) => titleCase ? char.ToUpper(ItemLabel[0]) + ItemLabel[1..] + "s" : ItemLabel + "s";

    private List<string> _allItems = [];
    private List<string> _matches  = [];
    private string _filterText = "";
    private bool _loadingItems = true;
    private bool _running;
    private string? _error;
    private int _doneCount;
    private readonly Dictionary<string, ItemResult> _results = new();
    private RunSummary? _completed;

    private double _progressPercent => _matches.Count == 0 ? 0 : 100.0 * _doneCount / _matches.Count;

    private DotNetObjectReference<BulkActionDialog>? _selfRef;
    private IJSObjectReference? _module;

    protected override async Task OnInitializedAsync()
    {
        try
        {
            _allItems = await LoadItemsAsync();
        }
        catch (Exception ex)
        {
            _error = $"Failed to load {Kind(false)}: {ex.Message}";
        }
        finally
        {
            _loadingItems = false;
            ApplyFilter();
        }
    }

    private void OnFilterChanged(string text)
    {
        _filterText = text;
        ApplyFilter();
    }

    private void ApplyFilter()
    {
        _matches = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_filterText)
            ? _allItems
            : _allItems.Where(n => WildcardFilter.ToRegex(_filterText).IsMatch(n)).ToList();
    }

    private async Task CopyErrorAsync(string? message)
    {
        if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(message)) return;
        await JS.InvokeVoidAsync("copyText", message, "Error copied to clipboard");
    }

    /// <summary>Runs (or re-runs — the button stays enabled after completion) the action
    /// against the currently filtered list, so a failed run can be repeated without
    /// closing the dialog or re-typing the filter.</summary>
    private async Task RunAsync()
    {
        _running = true;
        _error = null;
        _doneCount = 0;
        _results.Clear();
        _completed = null;
        StateHasChanged();

        try
        {
            _module ??= await JS.InvokeAsync<IJSObjectReference>("import", "./js/purge-stream.js?v=1");
            _selfRef ??= DotNetObjectReference.Create(this);

            var url  = new Uri(new Uri(Nav.BaseUri), Endpoint);
            var body = BuildRequestBody?.Invoke(_matches) ?? new { Action = ActionVerb, Kind = ItemLabel, Items = _matches };
            await _module.InvokeVoidAsync("run", url, body, _selfRef);
        }
        catch (Exception ex)
        {
            _error = $"{ActionVerb} failed: {ex.Message}";
            Notifications.Report(_error, ex, ActionVerb);
            _running = false;
            StateHasChanged();
        }
    }

    /// <summary>Invoked from purge-stream.js for every NDJSON line as it streams in.</summary>
    [JSInvokable]
    public void OnPurgeLine(string json)
    {
        var opts = new JsonSerializerOptions { PropertyNameCaseInsensitive = true };
        try
        {
            var evt = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<RunEvent>(json, opts);
            if (evt is null) return;

            if (evt.Done)
            {
                _completed = new RunSummary(evt.Total, evt.SuccessCount, evt.FailCount);
                _running = false;
                if (evt.FailCount == 0)
                    Snackbar.Add($"{ActionVerb}d {evt.SuccessCount} {Kind(false)} successfully.", Severity.Success);
                else
                    Snackbar.Add($"{ActionVerb} finished — {evt.SuccessCount} succeeded, {evt.FailCount} failed.", Severity.Warning);
            }
            else if (evt.Item is not null)
            {
                _results[evt.Item] = new ItemResult(evt.Success, evt.Message);
                _doneCount++;
            }
        }
        catch (JsonException ex)
        {
            Notifications.Report($"Malformed {ActionVerb.ToLowerInvariant()} progress line: {ex.Message}", ex, ActionVerb);
        }
        StateHasChanged();
    }

    /// <summary>Invoked from purge-stream.js if the fetch/stream itself fails.</summary>
    [JSInvokable]
    public void OnPurgeError(string message)
    {
        _error = $"{ActionVerb} stream failed: {message}";
        _running = false;
        StateHasChanged();
    }

    public async ValueTask DisposeAsync()
    {
        if (_module is not null)
        {
            try { await _module.DisposeAsync(); } catch (JSDisconnectedException) { }
        }
        _selfRef?.Dispose();
    }

    private sealed record ItemResult(bool Success, string? Message);
    private sealed record RunSummary(int Total, int SuccessCount, int FailCount);

    private sealed class RunEvent
    {
        public bool Done { get; set; }
        public int Index { get; set; }
        public int Total { get; set; }
        public string? Item { get; set; }
        public bool Success { get; set; }
        public string? Message { get; set; }
        public int SuccessCount { get; set; }
        public int FailCount { get; set; }
    }
}
FILE src/NxtUI.Web/Components/D3FlowGraph.razor
LENGTH 8600
@namespace NxtUI.Web.Components
@using Microsoft.Extensions.Options
@using NxtUI.Core.Configuration
@inject IJSRuntime JS
@inject IOptions<NxtUI.Configuration.RunsSettings> RunsOptions
@inject ILogger<D3FlowGraph> Logger
@implements IAsyncDisposable

<div class="bm-d3-flow-graph">
    @* D3 owns this inner div entirely — Blazor never touches its children *@
    <div @ref="_graphRef" class="bm-d3-graph-host"></div>
    <button class="bm-graph-fit-btn" title="Fit to view" @onclick="FitToViewAsync">
        <MudIcon Icon="@Icons.Material.Outlined.CenterFocusWeak" Size="Size.Small" />
    </button>
</div>

@code {
    [Parameter, EditorRequired] public Topology Topology { get; set; } = new();

    /// <summary>
    /// Whether the owning tab is currently focused/visible. When false, the
    /// canvas is hidden (display:none) and the D3 animation loop pauses
    /// entirely (§9 "Unfocused canvas consuming GPU" mitigation).
    /// </summary>
    [Parameter] public bool IsVisible { get; set; } = true;

    /// <summary>Fired when the user clicks the "Kafka" link in a pipeline row's popover.</summary>
    [Parameter] public EventCallback<string> OnOpenKafkaTopic { get; set; }

    /// <summary>Fired when the user clicks an instance row in a pipeline row's popover.</summary>
    [Parameter] public EventCallback<(string Service, string Server, int ProcessId, DateTime LastActivity)> OnOpenInstanceLog { get; set; }

    /// <summary>Fired when the user clicks the "errors" button in a pipeline row's popover —
    /// opens the errors dialog pre-filtered to that (service, pipeline). RunId is empty for a
    /// top-level node (this run's own events); non-empty for a node merged in from an
    /// expanded child run (docs/12 §7.4), so the errors dialog knows which run's own event
    /// store to filter instead of always assuming the page's own run.</summary>
    [Parameter] public EventCallback<(string Service, string Pipeline, string RunId)> OnShowErrors { get; set; }

    /// <summary>
    /// Resolves the log folder path shown as a tooltip when hovering an instance row's
    /// server name in the popover. Returns null if not (yet) known — a cache-only lookup,
    /// never triggers a filesystem search. (service, server, processId) -> path or null.
    /// </summary>
    [Parameter] public Func<string, string, int, string?>? ResolveLogPathHint { get; set; }

    /// <summary>Fired when the user clicks a collapsed child-run block (docs/12 §7.4).
    /// Currently opens the child run in a new tab, not an in-place expand — see the doc's
    /// note there for that follow-up.</summary>
    [Parameter] public EventCallback<string> OnOpenChildRun { get; set; }

    private ElementReference _graphRef;
    private IJSObjectReference? _module;
    private bool _previousIsVisible = true;
    private DotNetObjectReference<D3FlowGraph>? _selfRef;

    // RunDetail's mutable Tab parameter means every open tab's OnParametersSetAsync
    // re-runs whenever ANY tab's loading spinner toggles (TabService.NotifyChanged is
    // very chatty). Without this guard, every one of those unrelated notifications
    // re-serialized and re-pushed the whole Topology to JS. RunDetail creates a new
    // Topology instance only when it actually recomputes, so reference equality is a
    // correct, free way to detect "did the data actually change."
    private Topology? _lastPushed;

    protected override async Task OnAfterRenderAsync(bool firstRender)
    {
        if (firstRender)
        {
            try
            {
                _selfRef = DotNetObjectReference.Create(this);
                _module = await JS.InvokeAsync<IJSObjectReference>("import", "./js/d3-graph.js?v=47");
                var portConstraints = string.Equals(RunsOptions.Value.GraphPortConstraints, "FixedPos", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
                    ? "FIXED_POS" : "FIXED_SIDE";
                var edgeStyle = string.Equals(RunsOptions.Value.GraphEdgeStyle, "Curved", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
                    ? "CURVED" : "ORTHOGONAL";
                var defaultDirection = string.Equals(RunsOptions.Value.GraphDirection, "Vertical", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) ? "DOWN"
                    : string.Equals(RunsOptions.Value.GraphDirection, "Auto", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) ? "AUTO"
                    : "RIGHT";
                var layoutEngine = string.Equals(RunsOptions.Value.GraphLayoutEngine, "Custom", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
                    ? "custom" : "elk";
                await _module.InvokeVoidAsync("init", _graphRef, GetHandleKey(), _selfRef, portConstraints, edgeStyle, defaultDirection, layoutEngine);
                _previousIsVisible = IsVisible;
                await _module.InvokeVoidAsync("setVisible", GetHandleKey(), IsVisible);
                await PushTopologyAsync();
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                Logger.LogError(ex, "D3FlowGraph init failed");
            }
        }
    }

    /// <summary>Invoked from d3-graph.js when the popover's "Kafka" link is clicked.</summary>
    [JSInvokable]
    public Task RequestOpenKafkaTopic(string topic) => OnOpenKafkaTopic.InvokeAsync(topic);

    /// <summary>Invoked from d3-graph.js when an instance row in the popover is clicked.</summary>
    [JSInvokable]
    public Task RequestOpenInstanceLog(string service, string server, int processId, DateTime lastActivity) =>
        OnOpenInstanceLog.InvokeAsync((service, server, processId, lastActivity));

    /// <summary>Invoked from d3-graph.js when the popover's "errors" button is clicked.</summary>
    [JSInvokable]
    public Task RequestShowErrors(string service, string pipeline, string runId) =>
        OnShowErrors.InvokeAsync((service, pipeline, runId));

    /// <summary>Invoked from d3-graph.js right after the popover opens, to populate each
    /// instance row's server-name tooltip with its resolved log path (cache-only, no I/O).</summary>
    [JSInvokable]
    public string? RequestInstanceLogPathHint(string service, string server, int processId) =>
        ResolveLogPathHint?.Invoke(service, server, processId);

    /// <summary>Invoked from d3-graph.js when a collapsed child-run block is clicked.</summary>
    [JSInvokable]
    public Task RequestOpenChildRun(string runId) => OnOpenChildRun.InvokeAsync(runId);

    protected override async Task OnParametersSetAsync()
    {
        if (_module is null) return;

        if (IsVisible != _previousIsVisible)
        {
            _previousIsVisible = IsVisible;
            try
            {
                await _module.InvokeVoidAsync("setVisible", GetHandleKey(), IsVisible);
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                Logger.LogError(ex, "D3FlowGraph setVisible failed");
            }
        }

        await PushTopologyAsync();
    }

    private string GetHandleKey() => $"d3flowgraph-{GetHashCode()}";

    private async Task PushTopologyAsync()
    {
        if (_module is null) return;
        if (ReferenceEquals(Topology, _lastPushed)) return;
        try
        {
            await _module.InvokeVoidAsync("update", GetHandleKey(), Topology);
            _lastPushed = Topology;
        }
        catch (Exception ex)
        {
            Logger.LogError(ex, "D3FlowGraph update failed");
        }
    }

    private async Task FitToViewAsync()
    {
        if (_module is null) return;
        try
        {
            await _module.InvokeVoidAsync("fitToView", GetHandleKey());
        }
        catch (Exception) { }
    }

    public ValueTask DisposeAsync()
    {
        // _selfRef is a plain .NET object reference — cheap and synchronous, so it's
        // released immediately rather than deferred with the JS cleanup below.
        _selfRef?.Dispose();

        // Fire-and-forget: tearing down the D3/RAF state and JS module reference is
        // client-side cleanup for a container Blazor is removing from the DOM anyway
        // as part of this same tab-close. Awaiting the JS interop round trip here just
        // makes the tab visually linger until it completes, for no benefit.
        if (_module is not null)
            _ = SafeDestroyAsync(_module);
        return ValueTask.CompletedTask;
    }

    private async Task SafeDestroyAsync(IJSObjectReference module)
    {
        try { await module.InvokeVoidAsync("dispose", GetHandleKey()); }
        catch (Exception) { }
        try { await module.DisposeAsync(); }
        catch (JSDisconnectedException) { }
    }
}
FILE src/NxtUI.Web/Components/DiagnosticsMonitor.razor
LENGTH 2134
@* Debugging aid: monitors JS event-loop lag and frame rate.
   Disable via appsettings: "Diagnostics": { "Enabled": false } *@
@inject IJSRuntime JS
@inject ILogger<DiagnosticsMonitor> Logger
@implements IAsyncDisposable

@code {
    // How much JS event-loop lag (ms) triggers a Warning vs Info log entry.
    private const int WarnLagMs = 200;
    private const int InfoLagMs = 50;

    // Minimum FPS below which we warn.
    private const int WarnFps = 20;

    private DotNetObjectReference<DiagnosticsMonitor>? _selfRef;
    private bool _started;

    protected override async Task OnAfterRenderAsync(bool firstRender)
    {
        if (!firstRender || _started) return;
        _started = true;
        try
        {
            // Importing app.js ensures bmClientDiag is defined before we call it.
            await JS.InvokeAsync<IJSObjectReference>("import", "./js/app.js?v=8");
            _selfRef = DotNetObjectReference.Create(this);
            await JS.InvokeVoidAsync("bmClientDiag.start", _selfRef);
            Logger.LogInformation("Client diagnostics monitor started");
        }
        catch (Exception ex)
        {
            Logger.LogWarning(ex, "Client diagnostics monitor failed to start");
        }
    }

    [JSInvokable]
    public void OnClientMetrics(int lagMs, int fps)
    {
        var level = lagMs > WarnLagMs ? LogLevel.Warning
                  : lagMs > InfoLagMs ? LogLevel.Information
                  : LogLevel.Debug;

        Logger.Log(level, "client-diag | event-loop lag={Lag}ms | fps={Fps}", lagMs, fps);

        if (fps is > 0 and < WarnFps)
            Logger.LogWarning("client-diag | low frame rate fps={Fps} — UI thread may be blocked", fps);
    }

    [JSInvokable]
    public void OnLongTask(int durationMs, string attribution)
    {
        Logger.LogDebug(
            "client-diag | long-task blocked={Duration}ms | script={Attribution}",
            durationMs, attribution);
    }

    public async ValueTask DisposeAsync()
    {
        try { await JS.InvokeVoidAsync("bmClientDiag.stop"); }
        catch (Exception) { }
        _selfRef?.Dispose();
    }
}

                        <div class="bm-cs-row"><code>(…)</code>   <span>group / precedence</span></div>

                        <div class="bm-cs-row"><code>field:Load*</code>  <span>glob — * any chars, ? one char</span></div>
                        <div class="bm-cs-row"><code>field:=Value</code> <span>exact match</span></div>
                        <div class="bm-cs-row"><code>field:null</code>   <span>is null / missing</span></div>

                        <div class="bm-cs-head">Dates &amp; duration</div>
                        <div class="bm-cs-row"><code>today</code>        <span>start of today (UTC)</span></div>
                        <div class="bm-cs-row"><code>-7d</code>          <span>7 days ago · also -2h -30m -1w</span></div>
                        <div class="bm-cs-row"><code>2024-06-15</code>   <span>absolute date (UTC)</span></div>
                        <div class="bm-cs-row"><code>09:30</code>        <span>time of day (today UTC)</span></div>
                        <div class="bm-cs-row"><code>dur:&gt;00:01</code><span>duration &gt; 1 min (hh:mm)</span></div>
                    </div>

                </div>

                {

                        {

                    </div>
                }

                    </span>
                </div>

</div>

@code {
    [Parameter] public string  Value           { get; set; } = string.Empty;
    [Parameter] public EventCallback<string> OnFilterChanged { get; set; }
    [Parameter] public string? ParseError      { get; set; }
    [Parameter] public string? Class           { get; set; }
    // Null (the default) means "use the configured Ui:FilterDebounceMs" — set explicitly
    // per call site to override (e.g. TimelineTab's CSV import filter uses 250ms).
    [Parameter] public int?    DebounceMs      { get; set; }

    private int EffectiveDebounceMs => DebounceMs ?? UiOptions.Value.FilterDebounceMs;
    [Parameter] public string  Placeholder     { get; set; } = "Filter…";
    [Parameter] public bool    ShowHint        { get; set; } = true;

    [Parameter] public (string Aliases, string Label)[] FieldHints { get; set; } = [];

    private MudMenu?      _menu;
    private MudTextField<string>? _field;

    public async Task FocusAsync()
    {
        if (_field is not null) await _field.FocusAsync();
    }

    private async Task OpenFullReference()
    {
        if (_menu is not null) await _menu.CloseMenuAsync();
        _tabSvc.OpenOrFocus(TabModel.CreateFilterHelp());
    }

    private Task NotifyChanged(string v) => OnFilterChanged.InvokeAsync(v);

    private async Task ClearAsync()
    {
        Value = string.Empty;
        await OnFilterChanged.InvokeAsync(string.Empty);
    }
}
FILE src/NxtUI.Web/Components/GlobalErrorBoundary.cs
LENGTH 1354
using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components;
using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Web;
using Microsoft.Extensions.Logging;
using NxtUI.Web.Services;

namespace NxtUI.Web.Components;

/// <summary>
/// Wraps the main content area. When a component throws an unhandled exception:
///   1. Logs it with full stack trace.
///   2. Reports it via ErrorNotificationService (toast + Settings history).
///   3. Auto-recovers so the rest of the UI continues working.
///
/// Usage in Razor: <GlobalErrorBoundary>...content...</GlobalErrorBoundary>
/// </summary>
public sealed class GlobalErrorBoundary : ErrorBoundary
{
    [Inject] private ErrorNotificationService Notifications { get; set; } = default!;
    [Inject] private ILogger<GlobalErrorBoundary> Logger { get; set; } = default!;

    protected override async Task OnErrorAsync(Exception exception)
    {
        Logger.LogError(exception, "Unhandled component exception — recovering");

        var msg = exception.Message.Length > 150
            ? exception.Message[..150] + "…"
            : exception.Message;

        Notifications.Report(
            $"An error occurred and was recovered automatically.\n{msg}",
            exception,
            "Render");

        // Yield so the current render frame completes, then reset the boundary.
        await Task.Yield();
        Recover();
    }
}

            <MudProgressLinear Indeterminate="true" Color="Color.Primary" />

        else if (_error is not null)
        {
            <MudAlert Severity="Severity.Error">@_error</MudAlert>

                    {

                            <td>
                                <MudChip T="string" Size="Size.Small" Variant="Variant.Text"
                                         Color="@StateColor(g.State)">@g.State</MudChip>
                            </td>
                            <td class="@(g.TotalLag > 0 ? "bm-kafka-lag-warn" : "")">
                                @if (g.State == "Dead")
                                {
                                    <span>—</span>
                                }
                                else
                                {
                                    @g.TotalLag.ToString("N0")
                                    @if (g.TotalLag > 0)
                                    {
                                        <MudIcon Icon="@Icons.Material.Outlined.Warning"
                                                 Size="Size.Small" Class="bm-kafka-lag-icon" />
                                    }
                                }
                            </td>
                        </tr>
                    }
                </tbody>
            </table>
        }
    </DialogContent>
    <DialogActions>
        <MudButton OnClick="() => MudDialog.Close()" Variant="Variant.Text">Close</MudButton>
    </DialogActions>
</MudDialog>

@code {
    [CascadingParameter] IMudDialogInstance MudDialog { get; set; } = default!;

    [Parameter] public string TopicName   { get; set; } = string.Empty;
    [Parameter] public string Environment { get; set; } = string.Empty;

    private List<KafkaTopicConsumerGroup> _groups  = new();
    private bool                          _loading = true;
    private string?                       _error   = null;

    protected override async Task OnInitializedAsync()
    {
        try   { _groups = (await KafkaSvc.GetTopicConsumerGroupsAsync(Environment, TopicName)).ToList(); }
        catch (Exception ex) { _error = ex.Message; }
        finally { _loading = false; }
    }

    private static Color StateColor(string state) => state switch
    {
        "Stable"      => Color.Success,
        "Rebalancing" => Color.Warning,
        "Dead"        => Color.Error,
        _             => Color.Default,
    };
}

FILE src/NxtUI.Web/Components/KafkaTopicInfoDialog.razor.cs
LENGTH 798
namespace NxtUI.Web.Components;

public partial class KafkaTopicInfoDialog
{
    private static string FormatRetention(long ms) => ms switch
    {
        < 0                => "∞",
        0                  => "0",
        < 3_600_000        => $"{ms / 60_000}m",
        < 86_400_000       => $"{ms / 3_600_000}h",
        < 7L * 86_400_000  => $"{ms / 86_400_000}d",
        < 30L * 86_400_000 => $"{ms / (7L * 86_400_000)}w",
        _                  => $"{ms / (30L * 86_400_000)}mo",
    };

    private static string FormatBytes(long b) => b switch
    {
        < 1024                => $"{b} B",
        < 1024 * 1024         => $"{b / 1024} KB",
        < 1024L * 1024 * 1024 => $"{b / (1024 * 1024)} MB",
        _                     => $"{b / (1024L * 1024 * 1024)} GB",
    };
}

@namespace NxtUI.Web.Components
@using NxtUI.Web.Models

@if (_servers.Count == 0)
{
    <div class="bm-lb-empty">
        <MudText Typo="Typo.body2" Class="bm-muted">No servers configured for this environment.</MudText>
        <MudText Typo="Typo.caption" Class="bm-muted">Add servers to App:Environments[].Servers in appsettings.</MudText>
    </div>
}
else if (RootFolderMissing)
{
    <div class="bm-lb-empty">
        <MudText Typo="Typo.body2" Class="bm-muted">FileBrowser:RootFolder not configured.</MudText>
    </div>
}
else if (InitError is not null)
{
    <div class="bm-lb-empty">
        <MudText Typo="Typo.body2" Color="Color.Error">@InitError</MudText>
    </div>
}
else if (!Initialized)
{
    <div class="bm-lb-empty"><MudProgressCircular Indeterminate="true" Size="Size.Small" /></div>
}
else
{
    <MudTreeView T="LogFolderNode" Dense="true" Hover="true" Color="Color.Primary" Class="bm-lb-tree">
        <LogBrowserTreeNode Node="@RootNode"
                            Children="@RootNodes"
                            ChildCache="@ChildCache"
                            Expanded="@Expanded"
                            LoadingNodes="@LoadingNodes"
                            CheckedPaths="@CheckedPaths"
                            OnExpandedChanged="@OnExpandedChanged"
                            OnItemClick="@OnItemClick"
                            OnCheckChanged="@OnCheckChanged" />
    </MudTreeView>
}

@code {
    [Parameter] public List<string>        Servers        { get; set; } = new();
    [Parameter] public List<LogFolderNode> RootNodes      { get; set; } = new();
    [Parameter] public Dictionary<string, IReadOnlyList<LogFolderNode>> ChildCache { get; set; } = new();
    [Parameter] public HashSet<string>     Expanded       { get; set; } = new();
    [Parameter] public HashSet<string>     LoadingNodes   { get; set; } = new();
    [Parameter] public HashSet<string>     CheckedPaths   { get; set; } = new();
    [Parameter] public bool                Initialized    { get; set; }
    [Parameter] public bool                RootFolderMissing { get; set; }
    [Parameter] public string?             InitError      { get; set; }

    [Parameter] public EventCallback<(LogFolderNode, bool)>   OnExpandedChanged  { get; set; }
    [Parameter] public EventCallback<LogFolderNode>           OnItemClick        { get; set; }
    [Parameter] public EventCallback<(LogFolderNode, bool)>   OnCheckChanged     { get; set; }

    // Expose the server count for the parent's empty-state check
    private List<string> _servers => Servers;

    // Synthetic top-level node representing the configured RootFolder. Its children
    // are RootNodes (the caller's already-loaded merged top-level folder list across
    // all servers) rather than a ChildCache[""] entry — see LogBrowserTreeNode's
    // Children parameter.
    private static readonly LogFolderNode RootNode = new("Root", "", true);
}
FILE src/NxtUI.Web/Components/LogBrowserTreeNode.razor
LENGTH 3664
@namespace NxtUI.Web.Components
@using NxtUI.Web.Models

@* Recursive tree node — renders itself for each child, so tree depth is unlimited
   (the previous implementation hand-duplicated 4 nesting levels of markup, which
   silently capped browsable depth at 4; there was no MudBlazor limitation forcing
   that, just how the component happened to be written). *@

<MudTreeViewItem @key="@Node.RelativePath" T="LogFolderNode" Value="@Node"
                 Expanded="@Expanded.Contains(Node.RelativePath)"
                 ExpandedChanged="@((bool exp) => OnExpandedChanged.InvokeAsync((Node, exp)))"
                 OnClick="@((MouseEventArgs _) => OnItemClick.InvokeAsync(Node))">
    <BodyContent>@RenderRow()</BodyContent>
    <ChildContent>
        @foreach (var child in ChildrenOf(Node))
        {
            <LogBrowserTreeNode Node="@child"
                                ChildCache="@ChildCache"
                                Expanded="@Expanded"
                                LoadingNodes="@LoadingNodes"
                                CheckedPaths="@CheckedPaths"
                                OnExpandedChanged="@OnExpandedChanged"
                                OnItemClick="@OnItemClick"
                                OnCheckChanged="@OnCheckChanged" />
        }
    </ChildContent>
</MudTreeViewItem>

@code {
    [Parameter, EditorRequired] public LogFolderNode Node { get; set; } = null!;

    // Pre-supplied children for THIS node, bypassing ChildCache lookup — used only
    // for the synthetic "Root" node, whose children are the caller's already-loaded
    // top-level folder list rather than an entry keyed in ChildCache. Every other
    // (real) node looks its children up from ChildCache as normal.
    [Parameter] public IReadOnlyList<LogFolderNode>? Children { get; set; }

    [Parameter] public Dictionary<string, IReadOnlyList<LogFolderNode>> ChildCache { get; set; } = new();
    [Parameter] public HashSet<string> Expanded     { get; set; } = new();
    [Parameter] public HashSet<string> LoadingNodes { get; set; } = new();
    [Parameter] public HashSet<string> CheckedPaths { get; set; } = new();

    [Parameter] public EventCallback<(LogFolderNode, bool)> OnExpandedChanged { get; set; }
    [Parameter] public EventCallback<LogFolderNode>         OnItemClick       { get; set; }
    [Parameter] public EventCallback<(LogFolderNode, bool)> OnCheckChanged    { get; set; }

    private IReadOnlyList<LogFolderNode> ChildrenOf(LogFolderNode node) =>
        Children ?? (ChildCache.TryGetValue(node.RelativePath, out var ch) ? ch : []);

    // Row body: a selection checkbox on the left, folder name on the right — independent
    // of the expand/collapse arrow (MudTreeViewItem's own Icon/expand chevron), so checking
    // a folder never toggles its expansion and vice versa.
    private RenderFragment RenderRow() => __builder =>
    {
        <div class="bm-lb-tree-row">
            <div @onclick:stopPropagation="true">
                <MudCheckBox T="bool" Value="@CheckedPaths.Contains(Node.RelativePath)"
                             ValueChanged="@((bool v) => OnCheckChanged.InvokeAsync((Node, v)))"
                             Size="Size.Small"
                             Color="Color.Primary"
                             Class="bm-lb-tree-row-checkbox" />
            </div>
            <MudText Typo="Typo.body2" Class="bm-lb-tree-row-text">@Node.Name</MudText>
            @if (LoadingNodes.Contains(Node.RelativePath))
            {
                <MudProgressCircular Indeterminate="true" Size="Size.Small" Class="bm-lb-tree-row-spinner" />
            }
        </div>
    };
}
